message:
fix(database): extract MySQL packet header decoder with sequence validation and fix SQLite pool lifetime

MySQL driver (mysql.rs):
- Extract decode_packet_header() as a pure, synchronous function from
  the async read_packet() method, making header parsing independently
  testable without requiring a live connection or async runtime
- Add packet sequence number validation that returns a Protocol error
  when the received sequence ID does not match the expected value,
  catching stream desynchronization that previously caused silent
  data corruption or misleading error messages further down the pipeline
- Add three targeted unit tests: accepts matching sequence, rejects
  sequence mismatch with descriptive error, rejects oversized packets

SQLite driver (sqlite.rs):
- Fix critical pool lifetime bug: the static OnceLock was storing only
  the BlockingPoolHandle (a lightweight clone), causing the BlockingPool
  that created it to be dropped immediately after initialization, which
  put the handle into a permanent shutdown state and caused all subsequent
  spawn_blocking calls to fail silently or panic
- Change SQLITE_POOL from OnceLock<BlockingPoolHandle> to
  OnceLock<BlockingPool> so the pool remains alive for the process
  lifetime, with get_sqlite_pool() returning handle() on each call
- Add test_open_in_memory_exec_query_round_trip integration test that
  exercises the full open → create table → insert → query path using
  block_on to verify the pool is functional end-to-end

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
